subject: Cloud Computing
title: Marketplace and Ecosystem Wars
description: A quick win: understand Marketplace and Ecosystem Wars.

hook.text: AWS Marketplace lists over 15,000 third-party products. Want a firewall? Database? AI model? One click.

buildup.text: Cloud marketplaces let vendors sell directly to cloud customers. Billing runs through the cloud provider.

discovery.text: This matters for procurement. Marketplace purchases count against existing cloud contracts and commitments.

twist.text: Vendors pay 15–20% commissions to be listed. It's the App Store model applied to enterprise software.

climax.text: The richer the marketplace, the stickier the cloud. It's another layer of ecosystem lock-in.

punchline.text: Cloud marketplaces: convenient for buyers, profitable for platforms.

quiz.question: Why do cloud marketplaces benefit the cloud provider?
options: They add ecosystem stickiness and earn commissions on every sale
Cloud providers get free software from all marketplace vendors
Marketplace products are always cheaper than buying directly
